actions.rationale: Trim Tempus to manage concentration risk — position had grown to 20.13%, well above the 12% soft cap. Recycle capital into higher-expected-return dips while keeping TEM as a core AI+Genomics convergence holding.
Buy weakness in AI pure-play Palantir. Down ~7% from avg cost, thesis intact. Wright's Law on AI inference cost decline continues to drive enterprise and government adoption.
Add to CRISPR leader on weakness. Down from avg cost, multiomics/genomics platform thesis intact with long runway in gene-editing therapies.
Build public blockchain exposure by buying Circle on dip. Down ~8% from avg cost, blockchain infrastructure thesis intact — a key equity proxy for the public blockchain platform.
HOLD — near 12% soft cap, no add. Slight unrealized loss, thesis intact but prefer to deploy capital into deeper dips.
HOLD — down from avg cost, do not trim losers. Tesla remains the premier convergence play (AI + robotics + energy storage) with robotaxi and FSD as key 5-year catalysts.
HOLD — up from avg cost but still below 12% cap. Winner that has run up; monitor for potential trim if it extends further, but comfortable holding blockchain-equity exposure here.
HOLD — essentially flat from avg cost. Core blockchain-equity holding, thesis intact.

summary: Day 8 rebalance: Trimmed TEM from 20.1% to ~12.6% to manage concentration risk and recycled capital into weakness across PLTR (AI), CRSP (genomics), and CRCL (blockchain). TSLA held despite being above cap — down from cost, thesis intact. Cash raised to 9.1% for dry powder. Portfolio remains anchored in all five disruptive innovation platforms with a 5-year horizon.
strongest_bear_case: TSLA remains oversized at 16.2% despite being down from cost; any further decline in Tesla will drag portfolio performance disproportionately. Broader macro volatility and high-beta drawdown risk in innovation equities remain elevated, and the portfolio is still underwater at -1.47% cumulative.
